optimal_for: Full delta participation with no cap; best when vol is low relative to expected move size
major_risk: Elevated vol makes premium expensive relative to expected move; theta drag if the move is slow
structuring_guidelines: Strike at 25 or lower delta. Interesting if premium is less than 35% of potential profits

optimal_for: Target level is known; premium saving from sold strike is material; atmfsratio confirms the carry-to-cost is attractive
major_risk: Spot moves decisively through the sold strike — all further gains are forgone; worst outcome is being right directionally but too conservative on the target

optimal_for: Zero premium required; skew on the sold side is elevated (making the sold leg expensive) and the PM accepts the downside on the sold leg
major_risk: Sharp move against the view — sold leg generates continuous loss with no floor; requires margin; inappropriate when the PM has asymmetric conviction (strong upside view but no stomach for downside)
structuring_guidelines: Standard construction: buy 25Δ in direction of view, sell strike on the other side, targeting zero net premium. Increase leverage by reducing the delta of the strikes

optimal_for: Near-zero premium with a known target; sold wing on the opposite side is placed at a level the PM considers a credible floor/ceiling with tolerable loss if breached
major_risk: Spot trades through the sold wing — loss accrues beyond that level with no further hedge; sold option in the direction of the view caps the gain at the target
structuring_guidelines: Buy ATM forward. Sell in-direction leg at the target level (typically 25Δ–15Δ). Sell opposite-side wing to bring net premium to zero or small credit — wing strike chosen to match the funding need, typically 15Δ–10Δ.

optimal_for: Very high payout ratio when the PM has strong conviction the move will be directional and clean — spot will not trade through the knock-out barrier before reaching the target
major_risk: Spot touches the knock-out barrier at any point during the option life — the trade ceases to exist immediately, regardless of where spot finishes at expiry; path matters more than the final level
structuring_guidelines: Knock-out barrier set beyond the target in the direction of the view. Wider barrier = lower payout ratio but more resilient. Narrower barrier = higher payout ratio but fragile. Cross-check: barrier must be set above a level the market is unlikely to trade through before the target is reached. Payout ratio needs to be higher than 2.25 to be interesting

optimal_for: Near-zero premium with a known target; PM accepts being net short beyond the target level in exchange for paying no premium
major_risk: Spot moves well through the target — the two short legs leave a net short position above the target, generating losses that increase with spot; unlimited risk beyond target
structuring_guidelines: Buy 1 ATM forward option. Sell 2 options at the target level, sized so net premium ≈ 0. Max profit realised at the target. Beyond target the position is net short one option — confirm the PM understands and accepts this. Target should be a high-conviction level, not a vague estimate. Check that the short gamma above target is tolerable given portfolio context.

optimal_for: Fixed known payout if spot finishes through the strike at expiry; useful as a comparison to quantify the binary value of a threshold being breached
major_risk: Binary gamma near expiry makes the position extremely sensitive to small moves at the strike; all-or-nothing payout is difficult to manage into expiry; overpriced vs vanilla for large moves
structuring_guidelines: Strike set at the target level or at a key threshold (e.g. a round number or technical level). Payout fixed in USD notional terms. Premium typically lower than vanilla at same strike but payout is fixed — compare premium vs expected payout to assess EV. Looking for sub 13% premium to be interesting.

optimal_for: Lowest-premium binary expression; PM has strong conviction that spot will not trade through the knock-out barrier during the life and will finish through the digital strike at expiry
major_risk: Two independent ways to lose: spot touches the knock-out barrier at any point (trade dies immediately), or spot finishes on the wrong side of the digital strike at expiry; the combined barrier and binary risk makes this the most fragile structure
structuring_guidelines: Digital strike at the target level or key threshold. Knock-out barrier set beyond the target in the direction of the view — same logic as a standalone RKO. Premium materially lower than a standalone digital at the same strike. Use as a pricing reference to illustrate the cost of stacking barrier and binary risk; rarely appropriate as a primary trade.
